description: Note *Not available in 3DEC . The CYSoil model is a strain-hardening constitutive model characterized by a frictional and cohesive Mohr-Coulomb shear envelope and an elliptic volumetric cap with ratio of axes, defined by a shape parameter \(\alpha\) . The CYSoil model in FLAC3D 6 is an updated version of the FLAC3D 5 CYSoil model with the following built-in features: a cap hardening law to capture the volumetric power law behavior observed in isotropic compaction tests; a friction-hardening law to reproduce the hyperbolic stress-strain law behavior observed in drained triaxial tests; and a com

property_groups.name: Properties
property_groups.description: Material properties for the 'cap-yield' zone constitutive model. Assign the model with 'zone cmodel assign cap-yield [range ...]', then set these with 'zone property <keyword> <value> [range cmodel-name cap-yield]'.

properties.description: ultimate cohesion, c. The default is 0.0.
ultimate dilation angle, \psi_f. The default is 0.0.
ultimate friction angle, \phi_f. If the specified value is less than 0.1 degree, it will be limited to 0.1 degree for numerical stability.
mobilized friction angle, \phi_m, which should be initialized no less than the value determined by \phi^{nc}_m (see this equation) via a command or FISH function.
cap pressure, p_c, which can be initialized via a command or a FISH function.
initial mean effective stress, p_{ini}. Required if pressure-cap is not specified. It can be initialized via a command or a FISH function.
reference pressure, p_{ref}. A non-zero value should be specified based on the unit of stress/pressure adopted in the model.
current Poisson’s ratio, \nu. The default is 0.2.
dimensionless elastic shear modulus reference value, G_{ref}. A non-zero value should be specified.
tension limit, \sigma^t. The default is 0.0.
dimensionless cap yielding surface parameter, \alpha. The default is 1.0.
dimensionless calibration factor, \beta. The default is 1.0.
current mobilized dilation angle, \psi_m. It is read-only, except for table-dilation ǂ 0.
exponent for the pressure-dependent elastic moduli, m. The default is 0.5, and the upper limit is 0.99.
failure ratio, R_f. The default is 0.9.
If true, the tension limit is set to 0 in the event of tensile failure. The default is false.
= 0 (default), no cap. The elastic moduli are functions of the mean effective stress, and pressure_initial should be specified.
= 0 (default), the built-in Rowe dilation rule is used.
= 0 (default), the built-in shear hardening law is used.
initial mobilized friction angle, \phi_0 associated with zero plastic shear strain. By default, \phi_0 = \phi_m if \epsilon_{ps} is initialized to 0.0; otherwise, \phi_0 = 0.0 if not specified.
constant \phi_{cv}. The default value is calculated by this equation. Required for specification only if flag-dilation = 2.
over-consolidation ratio, OCR. The default is 1.0.
multiplier on current plastic cap modulus to give elastic bulk and shear moduli, R. The default is 5.0 with cap and 0.0 without cap.

maximum (upper-bound limit) of the elastic shear modulus, G^e_{max}. The default internally calculated value is 10 \times G^e_{ini}, where G^e_{ini} is the initial G^e when the model is first time set up.

minimum (lower-bound limit) of the elastic shear modulus, G^e_{min}. The default internally calculated value is 0.1 \times G^e_{ini}, where G^e_{ini} is the initial G^e when the model is first time set up.
accumulated plastic shear strain, \gamma^p. By default, it is initialized internally.
accumulated plastic shear strain, e^{pt}. The default initial value is 0.0.
accumulated plastic volumetric strain, e^p. By default, it is initialized internally.
name of the table relating cap pressure to plastic volume strain.
name of the table relating cohesion to plastic shear strain.
name of the table relating mobilized dilation angle to plastic shear strain.
name of the table relating mobilized friction angle to plastic shear strain.
name of the table relating tensile strength to plastic tensile strain.
initial void ratio, \hat{e}_{ini}. The default is 1.0.
allowable maximum void ratio, \hat{e}_{max}. The default is 999.0, a virtual value to bypass the dilatancy cut-off.
current elastic bulk modulus
mean effective stress, p
current elastic shear modulus
deviatoric stress, q
current void ratio, \hat{e}
current elastic Young’s modulus, E
